stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 15 subscribers. The sample mean is 84.2 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 28.3 Mbps. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 82.0 Mbps and standard deviation of 18.3 Mbps. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 84.2, 95% CI = [68.5, 99.9]. Without outliers: mean = 82.0, 95% CI = [71.8, 92.2]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [68.5, 99.9]. Point estimate: 83.1 Mbps.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [68.5, 99.9].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
